observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Establish a Disposal Committee to oversee and facilitate the proper disposal
of unserviceable properties of the MGB;
2. Direct the MGSO to securely store unserviceable properties to prevent
further deterioration;
3. Require the Municipal Accountant to assist the MGSO in preparing the
Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) by
providing the necessary information; and
4. Avail the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2020-006 in order to establish PPE balances that are verifiable
as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Direct the MGSO, with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer’s Office
(MEO), to complete the Road Map with all necessary details;
2. Instruct the MAO to ensure that the LRNLCs contain complete and accurate
information;
3. Task the MGSO with completing the LRNPCs, ensuring all essential details
are included; and
4. Require the IC to thoroughly prepare the RPCLRN, addressing any gaps in
information to facilitate proper verification.

Nevertheless, we recommended and Management agreed to continue its
adherence to this good practice and strong coordination between both offices.

We commend Management for its remarkable efforts exerted by the MAO
concerned personnel in ensuring full settlement/liquidation of the CAs granted.
Moreover, we recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Stop the practice of granting additional CAs to AOs with unliquidated CAs
in accordance with Section 89 of PD No. 1445; and
2. Increase the bonding requirements of bonded AOs with insufficient bond
pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 to protect the interest of the MGB,
otherwise ensure that CA granted is within the limit of the fidelity bond.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to promptly document all details of the DMs
and CMs processed by the depository bank immediately upon receipt of the
BS to facilitate the timely reconciliation of discrepancies;
2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the bank to determine the
nature of the unrecorded ₱200,000.00 deposit to properly account for it; and
3. Require the Municipal Accountant to address all reconciling items identified
in the BRS to ensure accurate reporting of the account balance in the FS.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the MAO to:
1. Make the necessary adjustments to reclassify the balances under the fund
transfer accounts into the appropriate accounts in accordance with Annex B
of COA Circular No. 2015-009 in order to come up with fairly presented FS;
2. Submit the FURs promptly to SAs in accordance with the terms and
conditions stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) or the
provisions under COA Circular No. 94-013, in case no reportorial
requirements are provided in the MOA;
3. Conduct periodic reconciliation of its records with the SAs to arrive at a
correct/reconciled balance of the fund transfer accounts;
4. Exert utmost effort in retrieving the necessary documents to support the
accuracy and validity of the fund transfer account balances;
5. Thereafter, request for the write-off of dormant accounts in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2023-008, if applicable; and
6. Ensure proper accounting of the utilization of fund transfers received through
accurate recording of transactions and proper maintenance of subsidiary
ledgers and supporting schedules.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant
to:
1. Observe strictly the proper recognition of liabilities, only when goods are
already delivered or services are rendered, pursuant to the above-cited
regulations;
2. Ensure that supporting documents are attached when recording accounts
payable to validate the legitimacy of the claim;
3. Verify the long outstanding obligations, and if found to have no valid or legal
claims against the MGB, cause the reversion of the same to the
unappropriated surplus of the GF; and
4. Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 98 of PD No. 1445 regarding the
reversion of long-outstanding payables.

We commend Management for its remarkable accomplishments in the efficient
utilization of the 20% DF. In line with this, Management expressed its commitment in
sustaining and further enhancing its best practices in programming, prioritization, and
implementation of PPAs to ensure the continued maximization of resources in
achieving meaningful socio-economic development and environmental sustainability
that will benefit the community.

We commend the efforts exerted by Management in implementing actions and
measures pertaining to all aspects of DRRM towards building a disaster-resilient LGU
and communities. Moreover, Management expressed its commitment in sustaining and
delivering PPAs effectively and efficiently.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entry to
revert the erroneous accrual of ₱300,684.69;
2. Secure the necessary supplemental appropriation from the SB for the NTA
differential to be allocated to the LDRRMF that will serve as basis in
facilitating the actual transfer of cash and recording of journal entries in the
books of the Municipality; and
3. Require the MDRRMO and the MAO to prepare and submit the Report on
Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis to the AT within the
prescribed period.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the
necessary journal entries to reclassify the affected PPE accounts in order to come up
with accurate and reliable accounts balances in the FS.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Encourage all staff involved in procurement, including department
representatives and members of the BAC Secretariat, to attend targeted
workshops and seminars on procurement processes and guidelines;
2. Require departments to clearly categorize and list specific items needed, with
well-defined quantities, specifications, and estimated costs, rather than using
lump-sum amounts, in their PPMPs; and
3. Establish a more structured and detailed APP by consolidating PPMPs from
each department with clear timelines, priorities, budgets, and specific
quantities and cost breakdowns for each item, while mandating timelines
within PPMPs and the APP for procurement milestones to ensure alignment
with operational needs and transparency.

We recommended and Management agreed to resort to hiring of casual or
emergency staff in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of CSC MC No. 40, series of
1998 and Section 211(b) of the GAAM, Volume I, in order to avoid hiring significant
number of JO/COS personnel.

Likewise, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Municipal Human Resources and Management Officer to:
1. Prioritize the formulation of a “Qualification Standards and Salary Rates for
JO/COS Positions” and to come up with an HRP to be submitted to the SB
for subsequent enactment of a resolution adopting the same;
2. Revise the existing policy guideline on the hiring of JO and COS personnel
and consultants to properly determine the necessity of hiring them and to
strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto; and
3. Furnish the MAO with copies of JOs/COS to be attached to the DVs and
submitted to the AT , pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Section
1.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Further, we recommended that the Committee on Civil Service and Professional
Regulations of the SB take appropriate action to address all matters pertaining to the
organization and management; personnel administration, position classification and
pay plan, staffing patterns; and creation of positions and policy formulation, in
connection with the observations noted in audit.

We commend the LSB for the substantial utilization of SEF thus, ensuring the
immediate realization of identified priority PPAs funded therefrom.
Nonetheless, we recommended and Management agreed to:
1. Follow strictly the prescribed timeline in the preparation of the annual SEF
Budget as provided in DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017;
2. Require the LSB to prepare a three-year program indicating strategic
prioritization policies in the allocation of the SEF to schools, prior to the
formulation and preparation of its Budget and furnish the AT for reference
and review purposes;
3. Support the SEF budget with plan of intended priority PPAs to be funded
out of the fund presented by allotment class and per school;
4. Require the Municipal Treasurer, the LSB and the MBO to coordinate
properly so that accurate figures are included in the SEF Budget
computation; and
5. Discontinue the practice of charging expenditures to the SEF that are not
aligned with its primary purpose, and strictly adhere to the regulations
governing prioritization of expenses chargeable to the SEF.

Furthermore, we reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and
Management agreed to:
1. Require the GFP to prepare and submit a supplemental or revised GPB to the
DILG Provincial Office whenever there are realignments or newly
implemented GAD-related PPAs not originally included in the approved
GPB. This ensures proper documentation, monitoring, and compliance with
the provisions of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMCs.
2. Use the HGDG or any appropriate gender analysis tools to planned GAD
PPAs of the Municipality to ensure that they consider gender-related issues.

We commend Management for continuously extending assistance to the National
Government through faithful compliance of duties as withholding and remitting agent
of taxes in accordance with tax laws, rules and regulations.
Also, we recommended, and Management agreed to the continued
implementation of the existing good practice in withholding and remittance procedures
and ensure continuous compliance with the existing tax laws, rules and regulations.

We commend Management for its continued compliance with existing laws,
rules and regulations on proper deductions and remittances of GSIS, Pag-IBIG and
PhilHealth contributions in accordance with Section 6 of RA No. 8291, IRR of RA No.
9679 and HDMF Circular No. 275, and PhilHealth Circular No. 0001 series of 2014,
respectively.
Also, we recommended, and Management agreed to continue implementation
of the existing good practice in withholding and remittance procedures and ensure
continuous compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation: We commended Management for duly incorporating all COVID-19-related PPAs in its AIP/LDRRMFIP/supplemental AIPs/MOA in compliance with rules and regulations governing COVID-19 fund receipts and utilization.  Moreover, we recommended that Management continue to comply with rules and regulations pertaining to the disbursement of COVID-19 Fund to related PPAs.

We commended Management for its notable work accomplishments relative to the utilization of the 20% DF.  Accordingly, the AT recommended that Management continue its improved practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing PPAs to optimize the utilization of the said Fund, thus attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.

We recommended that the LCE:
Seek authority from the SB for the LCE to donate the various PPE being used by public schools;
Thereafter, furnish the MAO of the SB Resolution and Deed of Donation as basis for dropping from the books of the subject properties in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-386; and
Coordinate and request formally from the PGS for the immediate donation and turn-over of the assets purchased and constructed in favor of the MGB, and thereafter, record the same in the MGB’s books.

In view of the noted deficiencies, we recommended that the LCE require the MT to:
Enforce strictly the daily remittance of collections from revenue collectors to avoid accumulation and possible loss of government funds through theft or misappropriation;
Regularly monitor the remittances of collections of the accountable officers to avoid delayed remittances; and
Continue to deposit all collections intact daily or not later than the next banking day to the AGDB pursuant to the pertinent regulations on the receipts and deposit of government monies.
Also, we recommended that the LCE instruct the MA and the MT to prioritize the reconciliation of the Cash – Local Treasury account to come up with the correct balance thereof.

We also recommended that Management:
Fast track the implementation of PPAs funded by the NGAs so that benefits attached thereto can be enjoyed by the recipients as soon as possible;
Return the unutilized amount of completed PPAs to concerned SAs per MOA; and
Comply strictly with the requirements of MOA and/or general guidelines on the utilization and reporting of fund transferred by various NGAs.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA to confirm the balances of all the Due to NGAs accounts and/or make representation to the concerned agencies to reconcile their respective records.
Otherwise, Management shall return the said unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury, if the documents or records of the specific accounts cannot be located or trace anymore.

We recommended that Management ensure that the identified priority PPAs are implemented as planned and to utilize fully the LDRRMF within the scheduled timeframes.  In such manner, the in-house capability of the LGU to manage efficiently all types of emergencies and to achieve orderly transitions from response to sustained recovery in case of calamity will be strengthened.

We recommended that LSB stop the practice of charging to SEF the expenditures which are not among the primary purpose of the establishment of the said fund, and strictly adhere to the regulations on the prioritization of expenses chargeable against SEF as provided under the aforementioned rules and regulations.

We recommended that:
Management communicate immediately to the MBO and other concerned offices any adjustment to be taken up in the agency’s records to correct financial reports and avoid confusion among prospective readers thereof; and
The concerned offices to revise and effect the necessary adjustments in their respective reports to present fairly the agency’s records.

Further, we recommended that the LCE:
Ensure that Municipal GFPS is fully functional and members are capable to promote gender mainstreaming pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM -NEDA JMC No. 2013-01;
Instruct the GFPS members to follow-up the approval of the Local GAD Code and expedite its publication; and
Direct the staff or members of the GFPS to attend GAD trainings to learn on how to use tools such as the HGDG and PIMME in the monitoring and evaluation of results of GAD PPAs that would aid in assessing the effectiveness and efficiency of the MGB’s gender mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) activate and instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of all properties of the Municipality and submit the corresponding RPCPPE within the prescribed period, thereafter be reconciled with the accounting records. For unserviceable property and obsolete PPEs, these should be reported in the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Property (IIRUP); and

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Supply Officer give priority in reconciling the PPE accounts with noted variances or discrepancies and to make the necessary adjustments, if any.

We recommended that Management adopt measures to maximize the utilization of the LDRRMF from current year’s appropriation and prior years’ unexpended balance so that the PPAs related to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness program may be implemented within the scheduled timeframes.

We recommended that Management continuously employ measures and use their available resources to optimize their compliance with RA No. 9003. Also, we recommended that Management expedite the completion of the Municipal SWM Plan and consider assigning additional personnel in the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources  Office.

We recommended that the LCE evaluate the current composition of Municipal SWM Board and look for possible competent members that will satisfy the condition under Section 5, Rule VI of the IRR of RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the provisions of the HGDG and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 be complied with by subjecting the GAD PPAs to the GAD Checklist during the project identification and designing programs to determine whether the same fully address gender related issues.

We recommended that Management maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through implementation of GAD related programs and projects that will address gender issues and concerns, recognize equality among men and women, and pursue women’s empowerment in the Municipality as prescribed under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that Management coordinate with the Sangguniang Bayan to expedite the enactment of updated Municipal Tax Ordinance and introduce new rates that would be responsive to the need of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require the business taxpayers to submit a Sworn Statement of its Gross Sales and/or Receipts for the preceding year as basis for the computation of business taxes due from them and if necessary, require them to submit their financial statements or income tax returns as additional supporting documents.

We commended Management for continuously extending assistance to the National Government through faithful compliance of duties as withholding and remitting agent of taxes in accordance with tax laws, rules and regulations.

We commended the Municipal government in its continuous compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deductions and remittance of GSIS premiums in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 8291.

We recommended that Management:
Remit immediately the long-outstanding balances and ensure timely remittance of the monthly dues to avoid the imposition of penalties, surcharges and interest arising from delayed remittances;

Enjoin the concerned personnel to update their accounts and fully settle their overdue or matured loans with the GSIS; and

Exhaust all means to pay the obligations of their officials and employees who will be separated from the service, such as utilizing their Terminal Leave Benefits to avoid further accumulation of interests and penalties.

We recommended Management in its continuous compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deductions and remittance of HDMF contributions in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9679.

We commended Management in its continuous compliance with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deductions and remittance of PhilHealth contributions in accordance with Section 18 of the Revised IRR of National Health Insurance Act of 2013 or RA No. 7875 as amended by RA No. 9241 and 10606.

Exert extra effort and obtain all the necessary documents needed to support the identified reconciling items and draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment/correction in the book;
Review all the long outstanding bank reconciling items and exert efforts to reduce the same and monitor the said reconciling items to ensure not to increase in the ensuing years; and
Prepare the BRS on regular basis and submit within 10 days from receipt of BS pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Submit the DTRs of the all the Job Order personnel for CY 2018 to support the payments made in the said year;
In the ensuing year, require all the JO personnel to prepare Accomplishment Reports in accordance with their job description as stated in the Contract of Agreement and submit the same to COA for audit together with their DTRs and payrolls; and
Execute the Contract of Agreements between the JO personnel and the Municipality to ensure the validity of services rendered by the said personnel covering a certain period.

We recommended and Management agreed to review the non-moving accounts to identify the source agencies, confirm the balances and take appropriate action whether to utilize or return the same. If review is not possible due to absence/insufficiency of records, the same should be requested for write-off in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that Management:
Cause the cancellation of stale checks with available records and prepare the necessary adjusting entries so that the Cash in Bank, LCCA and the corresponding Payable and Equity accounts balance will be fairly presented in the financial statements; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to exert extra effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks specifically those checks without the identified payees and if per record these have been stale for many years, consider the reversion of these payables to the unappropriated surplus.
Management Comment:
Management has agreed to comply with the audit recommendations. Also, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer assure the regular monitoring of all checks issued.

We recommended and Management agreed to enforce the collection of the loans granted to the BWD together with the corresponding interest in accordance with the MOAs entered into by the latter and the Municipality. Otherwise, resort to available remedies provided by law. In addition, Management should recognize the interest income in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management particularly the Accounting Department record all the transactions of the Municipality in accordance with the prescribed guidelines in the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards. Also, we recommended that the following correcting entries be effected:
Management Comment:
Management has agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the CY 2018 identified priority PPAs be implemented to better prepare for and lessen the impact of natural and man-made hazards/disasters and promote socio economic growth in the Municipality.

We recommended that Management through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board expedite the completion/updating of the 10-year Municipal SWM Plan and submit the same for review and approval by the NSWMC to serve as guide for the efficient management of solid waste generated within the Municipality’s jurisdiction.
Management Comment:
Management has agreed to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended that Management develop and enforce a policy that will require the Municipality’s constituents to segregate their waste products at source and enhance the awareness of stakeholders on the existing laws, rules, and regulations affecting solid waste management.
Management Comment:
Management has agreed to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS review the Actual GAD costs reported in the GAD accomplishment report and refrain from charging to the GAD Budget expenditures for projects without a well-defined gender issue to be addressed. We also recommended and they agreed that changes in the GAD Plan and Budget be communicated in writing to the DILG Office concerned.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
The MPDO to collate the sex-disaggregated data required for the Municipality’s major GAD projects, programs and activities; and
The GAD Focal Point System to allot budget for capacity building in the setting-up and maintenance of a GAD database
Management Comment:
Management has agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE issue an executive order establishing the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to facilitate the evaluation of the GAD-related accomplishments and the preparation of the GAD Evaluation Report.

We recommended that Management instruct the  OIC-Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to coordinate closely with each other in monitoring the implementation of projects funded from 20% Development Fund to attain the optimal socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes which is the core of the fund allocation.
We also recommended that Management prepare detailed plans and specifications before allocating the budget to the intended projects to ensure that the amount to be allotted is sufficient and to avoid delays in project implementation.
Management Comment:
Management has agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management study and implement how the pertinent rules and regulations governing the operation of the Municipal Slaughterhouse can be complied in accordance with RA No. 7160 and RA No. 9296 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations to ensure the protection of human and animal health against direct and indirect hazards and thereafter impose the collection of fees stated in the tax ordinance. Also, advise other meat and fish vendors to utilize the wet market section of the public market for the buyers and the sellers to have a common market place.
Management Comment:
Management has agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management:
Prioritize the updating of RPT records by providing enough manpower and equipment to the MTO so that said Office will be able to prepare all the needed reports for the establishment and collection of RPT and  SET Receivables;
Prepare a complete certified list of taxpayers based on RPT Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card so as to provide correct basis in setting up of the RPT/SET Receivables;
Post the list of taxpayers with unpaid taxes in the conspicuous places within the Municipality including its component barangays; and
Seek the aid of the barangay officials in the collection of RPT/SET by informing the taxpayers in each barangay of their responsibility of paying the tax.
Management Comment:
Management has agreed to comply with the audit recommendations.

We recommended that Management exert extra effort to reconcile the PPE balances presented in the financial statements with its supporting schedules and records and perform it regularly so that said accounts will be accurately presented.
